data.title: RBC downgrades Mosaic after surprise miss in phosphate output, stock tumbles
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets downgraded fertilizer maker Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) to sector perform on Friday after the company said its Q3 phosphate output of 1.7 million tonnes came in below the company's expectations due to mechanical issues.

Mosaic, which mines, produces, and distributes millions of tons of potash and phosphate products each year, fell 10% to $30.17 in early trading after the announcement. 

Mosaic's phosphate sales also came in at 1.6 million tons, trailing production due to shipment timing delays.

"We are disappointed in another surprise phosphate production cut and expect shares will be down significantly on the news," analysts said as they cut the price target to $30.00.

"We are concerned that phosphate operational issues may persist, and expect at least several quarters of strong operations may be needed to win back investor confidence."

However, analysts said they continue to see a potential upside case with Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) returning to nameplate phosphate production, which would allow the company to benefit from still-elevated phosphate prices, but this will require much better execution.

On the second-quarter earnings call, Mosaic discussed delays in asset health restoration activities within the phosphate segment.

While the company hit its production targets for several weeks after completing maintenance in August, unexpected mechanical issues at the Riverview sulfuric acid plant and utility interruptions at Bartow in mid-September led to a sharp drop in output.

As a result, third-quarter phosphate production volumes of approximately 1.7 million tons, while meaningfully higher than previous quarters, fell below management's expectations.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, this company has a Buy rating with a score of 3.89 out of 5. YTD, the stock is trading up 36%, compared to the broader S&P 500 market, which is up 14.5%.

data.title: TIAN RUIXIANG prices $3 million offering of ordinary shares
data.description:
• TIAN RUIXIANG (NASDAQ:TIRX) announced on Friday that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 2 million class A ordinary shares and warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of additional 4 million class A ordinary shares in a registered direct offering at a combined purchase price of $1.50 per share and accompanying warrants, resulting in gross proceeds of approximately $3 million, before deducting fees and other offering expenses.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of $1.50 per share, will be exercisable immediately upon issuance and will expire five years following the initial exercise date.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about October 14, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • TIRX -43.94% premarket to $1.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canaan mines fewer bitcoins in September
data.description:
Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) said on Friday that the crypto miner mined 92 bitcoins in September, compared to 98 in August.

Shares were +4.10% pre-market to $1.27.

The month-end bitcoins and ethereum owned by the company reached 1,582 BTC and 2,830 ETH.

Operating hashrate at the end of September reached 7.84 EH/s. Deployed hashrate was 9.30 EH/s.

The results were driven largely by continued progress across Canaan's U.S. and African sites.

data.title: Ford picks new European head; puts gas, hybrid, and electric business units under same leadership
data.description:
As part of its Ford+ transformation to strengthen its presence across Europe, Ford (NYSE:F) announced key leadership changes in its executive team.

Jim Baumbick, previously Ford’s (NYSE:F) vice president of Advanced Product Development, Cycle Planning and Programs, has been promoted to President, Ford Europe effective November 1. In his new role, Baumbick will lead the teams developing products relevant for the European markets and will report directly to Ford (NYSE:F) Vice Chairman John Lawler.

To replace Baumbick in his former role, the company has tapped Sam Basile who comes to Ford (F) from its competitor, General Motors (GM) where he served as the company’s vice president of Global Product Programs.

The company also expanded Bryce Currie’s role to Chief Manufacturing Officer, and Andrew Frick, currently the president of Ford Blue and Model e, will also assume the leadership role for Lincoln. This puts the company’s global retail business units for gas, hybrid and electric all under the same leadership.

Additionally, Charles Poon will succeed retiring vice president of Vehicle Hardware and Engineering, Chuck Gray. The company also announced the retirement of its vice president of EV programs, Darren Palmer. His replacement has not yet been named.

data.title: WORK Medical Technology Group receives additional 180 days to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
•  WORK Medical Technology Group (NASDAQ:WOK) and its subsidiaries in China announced on Friday that it has received a notification letter from Nasdaq, granting the company an additional 180-calendar-day period to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rules.
  • According to the letter issued by Nasdaq on October 7, 2025, WORK Medical is eligible for an additional compliance period until April 6, 2026, as the company continues to meet all other applicable listing requirements for the Nasdaq Capital Market, except for the bid price rule.
  • WOK -3.96% premarket to $0.0871.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ciena rises as Morgan Stanley upgrades on rising demand, margin optimism
data.description:
• Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) shares rose 2% in premarket trading on Friday as Morgan Stanley upgraded the telecom equipment firm, citing the belief that margin worries are overdone and continued strong network demand. 
  • “Our CIEN upgrade is late, as the name has run another ~40% since FQ3 (where it already saw a meaningful rerating),” analyst Meta Marshall wrote in a note to clients. “While we admitted on the FQ3 earnings that our cautious margin stance had been too conservative given the RIF they announced, subsequent capex data points / meetings with CIEN in Ottawa and ECOC have made clear that the demand picture is quite positive in the near term. While we still think optical pricing at some point will soften, that caution is more on the transceiver side. We could still see the same thing happen on 800ZR, but will likely take until later next year, making us less concerned in the near term. At 30x+, we can't get more positive, as pluggables is still a competitive market and around half the business is still exposed to telco, but do acknowledge data points are likely to be more positive in the near term, a positive for the stock.” 
  • Marshall raised her rating on Ciena to Equal-Weight from Underweight and upped the price target to $140 from $100.

data.title: S&P Dow Jones reports $10.6B U.S. dividend increase in Q3 2025, growth slows
data.description:
• S&P Dow Jones Indices reported a net increase of $10.6 billion in U.S. common dividends for Q3 2025, marking a slower growth pace compared to previous periods.
  • Dividend increases totaled $14.0 billion, up 43% from Q2 2025 but down 0.7% from Q3 2024.
  • Dividend decreases rose to $3.4 billion, a 46.1% increase from Q2 2025.
  • For the 12 months ending September 2025, total dividend increases were $57.5 billion, down 23.1% from the prior year.
  • Despite slower growth, analysts expect a record dividend payout for the full year, driven by anticipated economic stability and tax benefits for consumers.
  • SPGI +0.58% premarket to $490.47.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brent drops over 2% as Gaza deal eases mideast tensions
data.description:
Brent (CO1:COM) futures fell over 2% on Friday to $63.88/bbl as the risk premium faded after the Gaza deal, while WTI crude (CL1:COM) slipped 2.3% to $60.12.

"Crude oil fell as tensions eased in the Middle East...This presents a major step toward ending the two-year war that raised the risk of supply disruptions in the oil market," ANZ analysts said.

"This saw the focus move back to the impending oil surplus, as OPEC proceeds with the unwinding of production cuts," they added.

However, the losses were limited as the U.S. announced further sanctions on Iran. The U.S. Treasury Department has sanctioned more than 50 individuals, entities, and vessels that facilitate Iranian oil and liquefied petroleum gas sales and shipments from the country.

Analysts at Fitch’s BMI, meanwhile, have maintained their Brent crude price forecast at an average of $68 per barrel for 2025 and $67 for 2026, down from $80 expected in 2024. BMI expects oversupply to build in the coming quarters, pushing prices lower.

A key prop for oil prices has been the surge in Mainland China’s crude stockpiling in 2025, as lower oil prices have incentivized building strategic inventories in China.

"If oil prices remain in their current range, the storage build should remain in play for 2026. However, should China begin reducing its crude imports as storage capacity reaches its limits, although this level is unknown, any decline in China’s appetite for imported crude will weigh heavily on oil prices," the brokerage added.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp announces redemption of medium-term notes
data.description:
• U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) announced the redemption of its 5.727% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Senior Notes due October 21, 2026.
  • The notes, with CUSIP No. 91159HJH4, will be redeemed on October 21, 2025, at 100% of their principal amount, plus accrued interest.
  • Payments will be made via The Depository Trust Company.
  • USB +0.08% premarket to $47.14.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brussels looks closely into guardrails deployed by top U.S. tech firms to protect minors
data.description:
The European Commission has asked U.S. tech firms Snapchat (NYSE:SNAP), YouTube, Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL), and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to provide details on how they undertake age verification on their respective platforms as part of a probe under the EU's Digital Services Act.

The EC said on Friday it wants to scrutinize if the tech companies do enough to keep minors safe and enumerate the methods they use to prevent underage individuals from accessing illegal products like drugs and vapes or viewing harmful material, such as content promoting eating disorders, on their platforms. 

Snapchat was asked by the EC to provide information about how it prevents children under 13 from accessing its services and on the features it has in place to prevent the sale of illegal goods for children.

The EC asked YouTube for information on its age assurance system and more details on its recommendation algorithm, following reports of harmful content being shown to minors.

For the Apple App Store and Google Play, the EC wants to know how the two companies manage the risk of users, including minors, being able to download illegal or harmful apps, like gambling and nudify apps, and also understand how their platforms apply apps' age ratings.
